<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << packet_id;
        stream << username; // Length-prefixed string
        stream << scores;   // Count followed by elements
        stream << hexi::null_terminated(metadata); // Null-terminated string
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_packet(const NetworkPacket& packet) {
    hexi::allocator<std::byte> allocator;
    auto buffer = allocator.allocate(1024); // Initial buffer allocation
    hexi::stream_adaptor stream(buffer, allocator);
    
    packet.serialise(stream);
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    result.reserve(stream.size());
    for (size_t i = 0; i < stream.size(); ++i) {
        result.push_back(static_cast<uint8_t>(stream.data()[i]));
    }
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

    uint64_t user_id;
    uint8_t age;

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

std::vector<uint32_t> read_item_batch(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    uint32_t item_count = hexi::read<uint32_t>(buffer);
    std::span<const uint8_t> items_span(buffer.data() + sizeof(uint32_t), buffer.size() - sizeof(uint32_t));

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>

const uint8_t* get_read_pointer(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    return buffer.data();
}

uint8_t* get_write_pointer(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    return buffer.data();
}

struct BufferInfo {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo check_buffer_space(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    BufferInfo info;
    info.readable_size = buffer.size();
    info.free_space = buffer.capacity() - buffer.size();
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::binary_stream_reader stream(adaptor);
    
    // Peek the data type without advancing the stream
    uint8_t data_type;
    stream.peek(&data_type, 1);
    
    // Read the item count
    uint32_t item_count;
    stream.read(&item_count, sizeof(item_count));
    
    // Read the appropriate data type based on the preview
    if (data_type == 0) {
        // Integer data type
        std::vector<uint32_t> items(item_count);
        stream.read(items.data(), item_count * sizeof(uint32_t));
        return {data_type, std::move(items), {}};
    } else if (data_type == 1) {
        // Float data type
        std::vector<float> items(item_count);
        stream.read(items.data(), item_count * sizeof(float));
        return {data_type, {}, std::move(items)};
    }
    
    // Default case if data_type is invalid
    return {0, {}, {}};
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct UserData {

    std::string name;

        adaptor(user_id, name);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_serialise_packet(uint32_t header, const UserData& data) {

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>

struct MessagePacket {
    uint32_t header;

};

    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    uint32_t header;
    adaptor.read(&header, sizeof(header));
    
    // Create a buffer_adaptor for the remaining data
    auto remaining_data = std::span<const uint8_t>(adaptor.data() + adaptor.position(), adaptor.size() - adaptor.position());
    hexi::buffer_adaptor remaining_adaptor(remaining_data.data(), remaining_data.size());
    hexi::binary_stream stream(remaining_adaptor);
    std::string_view message = stream.view<std::string_view>();
    
    return MessagePacket{header, message};
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/dynamic_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
void write_to_dynamic_buffer(hexi::dynamic_buffer<block_size>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    // Write the header value
    buffer.write(&header, sizeof(header));
    // Write the data array
    buffer.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
struct BufferData {
    uint32_t header;
    std::vector<uint8_t> data;
};

BufferData<> read_from_dynamic_buffer(hexi::dynamic_buffer<1024>& buffer, size_t data_length) {
    BufferData<> result;
    
    // Read the header value
    buffer.read(&result.header, sizeof(result.header));
    
    // Read the data array of specified length
    result.data.resize(data_length);
    buffer.read(result.data.data(), data_length);
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/tls_block_allocator.h>
#include <hexi/detail/intrusive_storage.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
using tls_allocator_type = hexi::tls_block_allocator<hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>, allocator_count>;

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
void enter_thread_with_allocator(tls_allocator_type<block_size, allocator_count>& allocator) {
    allocator.enter_thread();
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
void exit_thread_with_allocator(tls_allocator_type<block_size, allocator_count>& allocator) {
    allocator.exit_thread();
}
```

**Explanation:**  
The solution assumes that the `hexi::tls_block_allocator` provides `enter_thread()` and `exit_thread()` methods to manage thread-specific initialization and cleanup. These methods are invoked within the respective functions to properly handle the thread lifecycle when using the TLS allocator.</answer>

    size_t alloc_count = 0;
    size_t dealloc_count = 0;
    
    // Initial state: all blocks free
    std::cout << "Initial free blocks: " << element_count << std::endl;
    
    // Allocate all blocks
    std::array<std::uint64_t*, element_count> blocks{};
    for (size_t i = 0; i < element_count; ++i) {
        blocks[i] = allocator.allocate();
        alloc_count++;
        std::cout << "After allocation " << i + 1 << ": free blocks = " 
                  << element_count - alloc_count + dealloc_count << std::endl;
    }
    
    // All blocks allocated
    std::cout << "All blocks allocated: free blocks = 0" << std::endl;
    
    // Deallocate half of the blocks
    for (size_t i = 0; i < element_count / 2; ++i) {
        allocator.deallocate(blocks[i]);
        dealloc_count++;
        std::cout << "After deallocation " << i + 1 << ": free blocks = " 
                  << element_count - (alloc_count - dealloc_count) << std::endl;
    }
    
    // Reallocate some freed blocks
    for (size_t i = 0; i < element_count / 4; ++i) {
        blocks[i] = allocator.allocate();
        alloc_count++;
        std::cout << "After reallocating freed block " << i + 1 << ": free blocks = " 
                  << element_count - (alloc_count - dealloc_count) << std::endl;
    }
    
    // Cleanup remaining blocks
    for (size_t i = 0; i < element_count; ++i) {
        if (blocks[i]) {
            allocator.deallocate(blocks[i]);
            dealloc_count++;
        }
    }
    
    std::cout << "All blocks deallocated: free blocks = " << element_count << std::endl;
}
```

This solution demonstrates the free list management by tracking allocations and deallocations, showing how the free list size changes over time. The output reflects the internal behavior of `push()` and `pop()` operations through the allocator's public interface.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/detail/intrusive_storage.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>
#include <cstring>

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> preview_data_without_consuming(const hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>& storage, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    size_t available = storage.readable_size();
    size_t copy_size = std::min(bytes_to_preview, available);
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(copy_size);
    
    if (copy_size > 0) {
        const uint8_t* data = storage.read_ptr();
        std::memcpy(result.data(), data, copy_size);
    }
    
    return result;
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
struct BufferStatus {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferStatus<> check_buffer_status(const hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<1024>& storage) {
    BufferStatus<> status;
    status.readable_size = storage.readable_size();
    status.free_space = storage.writable_size() - storage.write_offset();
    return status;
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
void reset_buffer(hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>& storage) {
    storage.set_read_offset(0);
    storage.set_write_offset(0);
}
```</answer>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>

    size_t capacity;
    size_t current_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo<> get_buffer_info(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, 1024>& buffer) {

    info.capacity = buffer.capacity();  // Get the total capacity from the buffer
    info.current_size = buffer.size();  // Get the current readable data size
    info.free_space = info.capacity - info.current_size;  // Calculate available free space
    return info;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> preview_buffer_data(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_to_preview);
    
    // Get the current read pointer and available data size
    const auto read_ptr = buffer.read_ptr();
    const size_t available = buffer.size(); // Assuming buffer.size() returns available bytes
    
    // Calculate how many bytes we can actually preview
    const size_t copy_size = std::min(bytes_to_preview, available);
    
    // Copy data without advancing the read pointer
    std::copy_n(reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(read_ptr), copy_size, result.begin());
    
    // Resize the result to the actual number of bytes copied
    result.resize(copy_size);
    
    return result;
}
```

**Explanation:**
1. **Buffer Access:** The function uses `buffer.read_ptr()` to get the current read position and `buffer.size()` to determine available data.
2. **Safe Copying:** It calculates the minimum between requested bytes and available data to avoid out-of-bounds access.
3. **Non-Destructive Copy:** Uses `std::copy_n` to copy data from the read pointer to the result vector without advancing the read pointer.
4. **Result Adjustment:** Resizes the result vector to match the actual number of bytes copied, ensuring accurate output.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
const std::byte* get_read_pointer(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer) {
    return buffer.data() + buffer.read_pos();
}

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::byte* get_write_pointer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer) {
    return buffer.data() + buffer.write_pos();
}
```

**Explanation:**
- The solution uses the `static_buffer` class from the `hexi` library, which manages a fixed-size buffer with read and write positions.
- `get_read_pointer` returns a const pointer to the current read position by adding the read offset (`read_pos`) to the base address of the buffer (`data()`).
- `get_write_pointer` returns a non-const pointer to the current write position by adding the write offset (`write_pos`) to the base address.
- The template parameter `buffer_size` allows flexibility in buffer size while maintaining type safety.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/stream_base.h>
#include <hexi/shared.h>

size_t get_stream_size(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    return stream.buffer_size();
}

bool is_stream_empty(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    return stream.buffer_size() == 0;
}

struct StreamInfo {
    hexi::stream_state state;
    size_t buffer_size;
    bool is_empty;
};

StreamInfo get_stream_state_info(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    StreamInfo info;
    info.state = stream.get_state();
    info.buffer_size = stream.buffer_size();
    info.is_empty = (info.buffer_size == 0);
    return info;
}
```

This solution correctly uses the `Hexi` library's `stream_base` interface to retrieve buffer information. The functions are implemented by calling appropriate methods on the stream object, ensuring proper encapsulation and adherence to the library's design.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << user_id << name;
    }
};

void serialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(writer);
}

void deserialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader, UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(reader);
}
```

**Explanation:**
- The `serialize_object` function calls `data.serialise(writer)`, which writes the `user_id` and `name` to the binary stream using the writer adaptor.
- The `deserialize_object` function calls `data.serialise(reader)`, which reads the `user_id` and `name` from the binary stream using the reader adaptor.
- This approach leverages the dual-purpose `serialise` method in `UserData` for both serialization and deserialization, as required.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <string>

void write_string_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::string& str) {
    writer.write(static_cast<size_t>(str.size()));
    writer.write(str.data(), str.size());
}

std::string read_string_from_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    size_t len;
    reader.read(len);
    std::string result(len, '\0');
    reader.read(result.data(), len);
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_container_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::vector<uint32_t>& container) {
    writer.write(static_cast<uint32_t>(container.size()));
    for (const auto& element : container) {
        writer.write(element);
    }
}

std::vector<uint32_t> read_container_from_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    uint32_t count = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    std::vector<uint32_t> result(count);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
        result[i] = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    }
    return result;
}
```

**Explanation:**
1. **Writing:** The count of elements is written first as a `uint32_t`, followed by each element in the vector.
2. **Reading:** The count is read first, then a vector of that size is created. Each element is read sequentially from the stream.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t value, const void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Write the integer value
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Write the byte array
    buffer.write(static_cast<const std::byte*>(data), length);
}

void read_data_from_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t& value, void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Read the integer value
    buffer.read(reinterpret_cast<std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Read the byte array
    buffer.read(static_cast<std::byte*>(data), length);
}

struct BufferInfo {
    std::size_t size;
    std::size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo get_buffer_info(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    return {buffer.size(), buffer.free()};
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void skip_bytes_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::size_t bytes) {
    buffer.skip(bytes);
}

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    MessageHeader header;
    buffer.read(&header, sizeof(header));
    buffer.skip(header.body_length);
    return header;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

const void* get_read_pointer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    return buffer.read_ptr();
}

struct BufferState {
    std::size_t size;
    const void* read_ptr;
    bool is_empty;
};

BufferState get_buffer_state(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    BufferState state;
    state.size = buffer.size();
    state.read_ptr = buffer.read_ptr();
    state.is_empty = (buffer.size() == 0);
    return state;
}

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void clear_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    buffer.clear();
    buffer.set_read_pos(0);
    buffer.set_write_pos(0);
}

void conditional_clear_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    if (buffer.get_read_pos() == buffer.get_write_pos()) {
        buffer.clear();
    }
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<typename T>
std::size_t encode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, T value) {
    std::size_t bytes_written = 0;
    uint64_t val = static_cast<uint64_t>(value);
    
    while (val > 0x7F) {
        uint8_t byte = (val & 0x7F) | 0x80;
        writer.write(&byte, 1);
        val >>= 7;
        bytes_written++;
    }
    
    if (val != 0) {
        uint8_t byte = val;
        writer.write(&byte, 1);
        bytes_written++;
    }
    
    return bytes_written;
}

template<typename T>
T decode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    T value = 0;
    size_t shift = 0;
    uint8_t byte;
    
    do {
        reader.read(&byte, 1);
        value |= static_cast<T>((byte & 0x7F) << shift);
        shift += 7;
    } while (byte & 0x80);
    
    return value;
}

template<typename T>
bool encode_decode_roundtrip(T original_value) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer writer(adaptor);
    
    encode_varint(writer, original_value);
    
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader reader(adaptor);
    T decoded_value = decode_varint<T>(reader);
    
    return original_value == decoded_value;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <cstring>
#include <cstddef>

using namespace hexi::detail;

bool check_regions_overlap(const void* src, std::size_t src_len, const void* dst, std::size_t dst_len) {
    auto src_start = reinterpret_cast<std::uintptr_t>(src);
    auto src_end = src_start + src_len;
    auto dst_start = reinterpret_cast<std::uintptr_t>(dst);
    auto dst_end = dst_start + dst_len;
    return (src_start < dst_end) && (dst_start < src_end);
}

bool safe_memory_copy(void* destination, const void* source, std::size_t length) {
    if (check_regions_overlap(source, length, destination, length)) {
        if (destination > source) {
            // Copy from end to beginning to avoid overwriting source data
            for (std::size_t i = length; i > 0; --i) {
                char* dest_ptr = static_cast<char*>(destination) + i - 1;
                const char* src_ptr = static_cast<const char*>(source) + i - 1;
                *dest_ptr = *src_ptr;
            }
        } else {
            // Copy from beginning to end
            for (std::size_t i = 0; i < length; ++i) {
                char* dest_ptr = static_cast<char*>(destination) + i;
                const char* src_ptr = static_cast<const char*>(source) + i;
                *dest_ptr = *src_ptr;
            }
        }
    } else {
        // No overlap, use efficient memcpy
        std::memcpy(destination, source, length);
    }
    return true;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Serialize packet_id as varint
    hexi::serialize(buffer, packet.packet_id);
    
    // Serialize tags vector with prefixed_varint
    hexi::serialize(buffer, hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.tags));
    
    // Serialize data string with prefixed_varint
    hexi::serialize(buffer, hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.data));
    
    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet;
    hexi::deserializer deserializer(buffer);
    
    // Deserialize packet_id
    deserializer >> packet.packet_id;
    
    // Deserialize tags vector using prefixed_varint
    deserializer >> hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.tags);
    
    // Deserialize data string using prefixed_varint
    deserializer >> hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.data);
    
    return packet;
}
```</answer>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    
    // Write header
    hexi::write(packet.header, result);
    
    // Write data length and data
    uint16_t data_len = static_cast<uint16_t>(packet.data.length());
    hexi::write(data_len, result);
    hexi::write(packet.data, result);
    
    // Add 16 bytes of padding (0x00)
    auto padding = hexi::fill<16>(0x00);
    result.insert(result.end(), padding.begin(), padding.end());
    
    // Write footer
    hexi::write(packet.footer, result);
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    // Default constructor required by block_allocator
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> objects;
    
    // Allocate and construct first object
    void* mem1 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem1) PoolObject(1, 1.5);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem1));
    
    // Allocate and construct second object  
    void* mem2 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem2) PoolObject(2, 2.5);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem2));
    
    // Allocate and construct third object
    void* mem3 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem3) PoolObject(3, 3.5);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem3));
    
    return objects;
}
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Memory Allocation**: The function uses the provided `block_allocator` to allocate memory for each `PoolObject`. The allocator manages memory in blocks of 10 objects for efficiency.

2. **Object Construction**: Placement new is used to construct each `PoolObject` in the allocated memory. This ensures proper initialization of the object's members.

3. **Pointer Storage**: Pointers to the constructed objects are stored in a `vector` and returned to the caller.

4. **Lifetime Management**: The caller is responsible for deallocating the memory using the allocator's `deallocate` method when the objects are no longer needed. This follows RAII principles while respecting the custom allocator's memory management strategy.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct TypedData {
    uint32_t id;
    int16_t value;
    double ratio;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_typed_data(const TypedData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor.write(data.id);
    adaptor.write(data.value);
    adaptor.write(data.ratio);
    return buffer;
}

TypedData read_typed_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    TypedData data;
    adaptor.read(&data.id);
    adaptor.read(&data.value);
    adaptor.read(&data.ratio);
    return data;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream;
    
    // Convert message_id to big-endian and write
    auto be_msg_id = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.message_id);
    stream.write(be_msg_id);
    
    // Convert each data_value to big-endian and write the container
    std::vector<uint16_t> be_data_values;
    for (auto val : packet.data_values) {
        be_data_values.push_back(hexi::endian::native_to_big(val));
    }
    stream.write_container(be_data_values);
    
    // Convert checksum to big-endian and write
    auto be_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);
    stream.write(be_checksum);
    
    return stream.get_buffer();
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    ProtocolPacket packet;
    
    // Read message_id and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t be_msg_id;
    stream.read(be_msg_id);
    packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_msg_id);
    
    // Read data_values count and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t count_be;
    stream.read(count_be);
    uint32_t count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(count_be);
    
    // Read data_values and convert each element from big-endian to native
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    stream.read_container(data_values, count);
    for (auto& val : data_values) {
        val = hexi::endian::big_to_native(val);
    }
    packet.data_values = std::move(data_values);
    
    // Read checksum and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t be_checksum;
    stream.read(be_checksum);
    packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_checksum);
    
    return packet;
}

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Extract the first 4 bytes as big-endian uint32_t
    uint32_t be_message_id = (static_cast<uint32_t>(buffer[0]) << 24) |
                             (static_cast<uint32_t>(buffer[1]) << 16) |
                             (static_cast<uint32_t>(buffer[2]) << 8) |
                             static_cast<uint32_t>(buffer[3]);
    
    // Convert to native endianness
    uint32_t native_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_message_id);
    
    return {native_id};
}
```</answer>
